title: Antibiotic resistance in animals - Canada.ca
content:
## Antibiotic resistance in animals
Learn about antimicrobial resistance related to food-producing animals and companion animals (pets). Also learn about the actions we are taking to address this problem.

## Services and information

### Antimicrobial resistance and animals - Actions
How we are taking action to address antimicrobial resistance related to the use of antimicrobials in animals.

### Post-Market Re-Evaluation of Medically Important Antimicrobials (MIAs)
How we assess if MIAs used in veterinary medicine may contribute to AMR in humans, and the risk management measures that can be taken.

### Personal importation of drugs for food-producing animals
How we are controlling personal importation of certain drugs for food-producing animals.

### Responsible use of Medically Important Antimicrobials in animals
How we are taking action to promote the responsible use of antimicrobials in animals.

### Lists Incorporated by Reference
Find information on regulatory List A, List B and List C.

### Veterinary Health Products (VHPs)
How we are regulating VHPs for the general health and welfare of companion and food-producing animals.

### Veterinary antimicrobial sales reporting
How we are collecting veterinary antimicrobial sales information to support our surveillance program.

### Antimicrobial Resistance and animals: Background
About the issue of antimicrobial resistance related to veterinary drugs and previous actions.

### Oversight on the quality of active pharmaceutical ingredients for veterinary use in Canada
We are increasing the oversight on the quality of active pharmaceutical ingredients for veterinary use in Canada.
- Public Health Agency of Canada

### Laws and regulations
- Final Publication in Canada Gazette, Part II: Regulations amending the Food and Drug Regulations (Veterinary Drugs – Antimicrobial Resistance)
- Health of Animals Act

- Antimicrobial Resistance and Use in Canada: A Federal Framework for Action
- The Canadian Integrated Program for Antimicrobial Resistance Surveillance (CIPARS)
- Canadian Institutes of Health Research Antimicrobial Resistance Initiatives
